title: Leobordea hirsuta
taxonomy: Leobordea hirsuta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Leobordea and is a type of succulent plant. This species is native to Madagascar and is known for its unique hairy stems and leaves. The classification of Leobordea hirsuta places it within the order Gentianales, which includes a variety of flowering plants. Further classification at the family level also places it within the Apocynaceae family, which is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family.
